children.name: hwWlanApPowerInsufficientImpact
children.description: This object indicates the impact of AP power insufficient.

children.name: hwWlanApOpticalTxPower
children.description: Transmit power of the AP's optical module.

children.name: hwWlanSlotNum
children.description: Description.

children.name: hwWlanApNotifyRadioId
children.description: Radio ID used to report alarms.

children.name: hwApPoePortPriority
children.description: This object identifies the priority of an interface.The default value is low.

children.name: hwWlanApOpticalVoltage
children.description: Voltage of an AP's optical module.

children.name: hwWlanApTemperatureType
children.description: This object indicates the AP temperature type.

children.name: hwApPowerFaultId
children.description: This object indicates the AP power fault ID.

children.name: hwAPDiskThresholdCurrent
children.description: The current Disk usage of an AP.

children.name: hwLowerEmdiSessionThreshold
children.description: This object indicates the lower eMDI session threshold on th AP.

children.name: hwApEntityPhysicalName
children.description: This object identifies the phisical name of entity name.

children.name: hwWlanStaAuthFailCause
children.description: The cause of the STA authentication failure.

children.name: hwWlanApCpuOverloadDescInfo
children.description: The high AP CPU usage.

children.name: hwWlanApWiredPortSupportedMaxRate
children.description: This object indicates the supported max rate of wired port.

children.name: hwWlanApUnAuthorizedApRecordNumber
children.description: This object indicates that unauthorized AP alarms exist.

children.name: hwWlanApIfIndex
children.description: AP interface index.

children.name: hwWlanRTURemainDays
children.description: This object indicates the AP RTU license remaining days.

children.name: hwApStorageName
children.description: This object identifies the name of Storage.

children.name: hwWlanApFaultID
children.description: This object indicates an FMEA fault alarm.

children.name: hwWlanApCpuOccupancyRate
children.description: AP CPU usage.

children.name: hwWlanCrcErrActual
children.description: CRC error alarm threshold value.The unit is 1/10000.

children.name: hwWlanApCfgCountryCode
children.description: Country code.

children.name: hwWlanApArpCfgRateThreshold
children.description: Description.

children.name: hwWlanApFaultInfo
children.description: AP fault description.

children.name: hwUpperSipfpmFlowThreshold
children.description: This object indicates the upper iPCA flow threshold on th AP.

children.name: hwWlanCrcPortID
children.description: CRC port number.

children.name: hwApPowerId
children.description: This object indicates the power ID of the AP.

children.name: hwWlanApPermitStaNum
children.description: The number of STAs allowed to associate with an AP.

children.name: hwWlanApArpAttackSrcMac
children.description: Description.

children.name: hwSubFirmwareName
children.description: AP's sub-firmware name.

children.name: hwWlanApTypeRadioTable
children.description: This table is used to query radio-related attributes of APs of a certain type.

children.name: hwWlanApTypeRadioEntry
children.description: The indexes of this table are hwWlanApType and hwWlanApTypeRadioIndex.

children.name: hwWlanApTypeRadioIndex
children.description: This object indicates the radio index of APs of a certain type.

children.name: hwWlanApTypeRadioMaxVAPNum
children.description: This object indicates the maximum number of VAPs that a radio supports.

children.name: hwWlanApTypeUndefinedTable
children.description: This table is used to operate the undefined AP type record reported by an AP.

children.name: hwWlanApOfflineReasonTable
children.description: This table is used to get AP offline reason counts.

children.name: hwWlanApWiredPortStatTable
children.description: This table is used to query or clear statistics on AP wired interfaces.

children.name: hwWlanWceApTable
children.description: This table is used to add, delete, modify, reset, or confirm APs. You can also use the table to query the configuration and restore factory settings of APs.

children.name: hwWlanApWiredPortLldpRemManAddrTable
children.description: This table is used to query management addresses of LLDP neighbors of a specified AP. No entry can be created, modified, or deleted in this table.

children.name: hwWlanApWiredPortLldpRemManAddrEntry
children.description: The indexes of this table are hwWlanApMac, hwWlanApWiredPortLldpRemLocalPortNum, hwWlanApWiredPortLldpRemIndex, hwWlanApWiredPortLldpRemManAddrSubtype, and hwWlanApWiredPortLldpRemManAddr.

children.name: hwWlanApWiredPortLldpRemManAddr
children.description: This object indicates the remote management address of the wired interface.

children.name: hwWlanApWiredPortLldpTable
children.description: This table is used to query LLDP neighbor information of a specified AP. No entry can be created, modified, or deleted in this table.

children.name: hwWlanApOnlineFailReasonTable
children.description: This table is used to get AP online fail reason counts.

children.name: hwWlanApTable
children.description: This table is used to add, delete, modify, reset, or confirm APs. You can also use the table to query the configuration and restore factory settings of APs.

children.name: hwWlanApOfflineTable
children.description: This table is used to query or clear AP offline records, including offline time and reason.

children.name: hwWlanApPingTimeOut
children.description: This object indicates the timeout period for an ECHO-RESPONSE after an ECHO-REQUEST is sent.Unit: ms Default value: 2000.

children.name: hwWlanApPingCount
children.description: This object indicates the number of times ICMP ECHO-REQUEST packets are sent.Default value: 4.

children.name: hwWlanApPingPacketSize
children.description: This object indicates the length of an ECHO-REQUE.

children.name: hwWlanApPingWaitTime
children.description: This object indicates the time to wait before sending the next ICMP Request packet.Unit: ms Default value: 2000.

children.name: hwWlanApPingResultFailureCount
children.description: This object indicates the number of AP ping operation failures. Only the result of the latest AP ping operation is kept.

children.name: hwWlanApPingResultMinResponseTime
children.description: This object indicates the minimum response time for an AP ping operation. Only the result of the latest AP ping operation is kept.Unit: ms.

children.name: hwWlanApPingApMac
children.description: This object specifies the MAC address in an AP ping operation. If the AP ID and MAC address are specified concurrently, the AP is pinged based on the MAC address.

children.name: hwWlanApPingResultSuccessCount
children.description: This object indicates the number of successful AP ping operations. Only the result of the latest AP ping operation is kept.

children.name: hwWlanApOnlineFailTable
children.description: This table is used to query or clear AP online failure records, including failure time and reason.

children.name: hwWlanApLicAuth
children.description:
Used to control whether the AP function can be enabled for users.
             If it is set to enable, the AP function can be enabled for users.
             Otherwise, the wlan AP function cannot be enabled for users.
             Options:
             1. enable(1)     -Enable Ap license switch
             2. disable(2)    -Disable Ap license switch
             default: disable(2)
